findings.title: Embedded private key material
findings.description: A private key is embedded directly in the firmware image, typically shared across an entire product line.
findings.recommendation: Provision a unique key per device via a hardware secure element; never ship a shared private key.

findings.title: Weak password hash format (MD5-crypt)
findings.description: Password hashes use the legacy MD5-crypt ($1$) format, which is fast to brute-force offline.
findings.recommendation: Use bcrypt/scrypt/argon2 with a per-user salt for credential storage.

findings.title: Weak password hash format (MD5-crypt)
findings.description: Password hashes use the legacy MD5-crypt ($1$) format, which is fast to brute-force offline.
findings.recommendation: Use bcrypt/scrypt/argon2 with a per-user salt for credential storage.

findings.title: Hardcoded credential or secret
findings.description: A hardcoded password, secret, or API key is embedded directly in firmware source/config.
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded secrets; provision unique per-device credentials via a secure element/HSM at manufacturing time.

findings.title: Hardcoded credential or secret
findings.description: A hardcoded password, secret, or API key is embedded directly in firmware source/config.
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded secrets; provision unique per-device credentials via a secure element/HSM at manufacturing time.

findings.title: Telnet daemon enabled at boot
findings.description: Firmware starts a telnet service, which transmits credentials and commands in cleartext.
findings.recommendation: Disable telnet; use SSH with key-based auth restricted to a management VLAN.

findings.title: Cleartext HTTP endpoint referenced
findings.description: Firmware communicates with a remote endpoint (e.g. update server) over unencrypted HTTP.
findings.recommendation: Use HTTPS/TLS with certificate pinning for firmware update and telemetry channels.

findings.title: World-writable file in firmware image
findings.description: File is world-writable, allowing any local process/user to modify it.

findings.title: Memory-unsafe or command-injection-prone function
findings.description: Firmware source calls a function commonly associated with buffer overflows or command injection.
findings.recommendation: Replace with bounds-checked equivalents (strncpy/snprintf); never pass untrusted input to system().

findings.title: Memory-unsafe or command-injection-prone function
findings.description: Firmware source calls a function commonly associated with buffer overflows or command injection.
findings.recommendation: Replace with bounds-checked equivalents (strncpy/snprintf); never pass untrusted input to system().

findings.title: Heartbleed (CVE-2014-0160)
findings.description: Bundled OpenSSL version is vulnerable to Heartbleed, allowing remote memory disclosure (private keys, credentials, session data).
findings.recommendation: Upgrade OpenSSL to a release newer than 1.0.1g.

findings.title: BusyBox wget command handling issue (CVE-2018-1000517)
findings.description: Bundled BusyBox version is affected by a known wget-related file handling vulnerability.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade BusyBox to a release newer than 1.28.0.

findings.title: Dropbear SSH memory corruption (CVE-2016-7406)
findings.description: Bundled Dropbear SSH version is affected by known memory-corruption vulnerabilities reachable pre-auth.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade Dropbear SSH to a release newer than 2016.74.

findings.title: Insecure/high-risk service exposed: Telnet (port 23)
findings.description: Telnet transmits credentials/commands in cleartext and is the #1 IoT botnet (e.g. Mirai) entry point.
findings.evidence:
BusyBox v1.19.4 (2014-01-01) built-in shell
IPCAM-DVR login:
findings.recommendation: Disable Telnet unless strictly required; otherwise restrict to an authenticated management VLAN.

findings.title: Insecure/high-risk service exposed: MQTT (port 1883)
findings.description: MQTT broker reachable; if unauthenticated, any client can publish/subscribe to device topics.
findings.recommendation: Disable MQTT unless strictly required; otherwise restrict to an authenticated management VLAN.

findings.title: Open network service: BaseHTTPServer (port 8080)
findings.description: Service BaseHTTPServer 0.6 is reachable over the network.
findings.recommendation: Confirm this service is required and reachable only from authorized management hosts.

findings.title: Web admin panel advertises default credentials
findings.description: The device's web management interface response references default/shipped credentials.
findings.recommendation: Force a credential change on first boot; never ship a working default account.
